title: gonolobus guatemalensis
intro: Gonolobus guatemalensis, also known as the Guatemalan milk vine, is a species of flowering plant in the milkweed family (Apocynaceae) native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gonolobus guatemalensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds, as well as acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's roots to create a tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
